title: Use skeleton screens instead of spinners for content areas
summary: AI-generated loading states default to centered spinners rather than skeleton screens that improve perceived performance.
instruction: Use skeleton screens approximating final content layout for content loading. Reserve spinners for whole-page loading or actions that cannot be approximated.
rationale: Research shows skeleton screens reduce perceived loading time by providing visual structure. Users are less likely to abandon a loading state when they can anticipate the content shape.

violations: A dashboard showing a centered spinner on page load with no layout hints.
A data table showing a full-page spinner while fetching rows.
controls: A dashboard with skeleton cards matching final layout - grey rectangles approximating chart positions.
A content feed with skeleton items showing avatar circle, title line, description lines.
borderline: A full-page route transition where content structure changes completely may justify a brief spinner.

exceptions.description: Operations completing in under 300ms (no loading state needed). Upload progress bars. Legacy systems where skeleton approximation is impractical.

detector.signalDescription: Find centered spinner elements. Check for nearby skeleton placeholder elements (grey dimmed rectangles, pulsing blocks).
limitations: Cannot detect loading states implemented entirely in JavaScript.
Button-level spinners (saving states) are appropriate and should not be flagged.

scenarios: Simple action button showing spinner during save - appropriate for button-level feedback.
Brand launch screen animation before app loads.
mitigations: Target content-area loading states, not button-level spinners. Flag only page-level spinner as sole treatment.

fixturePlan.description: A dashboard page with a single centered spinner when data is loading, no skeleton.
Same page with 4 skeleton cards matching final metric card layout with shimmer.
A page with skeleton header but spinner in content area because content structure varies.
